summary: Bank Transfer E2E and Marketplace Card Redesign Testing - Iteration 202. Backend: 10/10 tests passed covering guest checkout, payment proof submission, admin queue visibility, admin approve/reject flows, payer_name vs customer_name separation. Frontend: Public and in-account marketplaces both use ProductCardCompact with uniform card heights, fixed-height price/action blocks, promo badges working correctly. Fixed a bug in live_commerce_service.py where guest order customer_name was showing as '-' instead of actual name.
seed_data_creation: None - tests create their own test orders with unique IDs
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bank Transfer E2E flow is complete and verified. Key points: 1) POST /api/public/checkout creates guest order with customer_name, customer_email, customer_phone. 2) POST /api/public/payment-proof creates payment proof with payer_name (separate from customer_name). 3) GET /api/admin/payments/queue returns guest proofs with correct customer_name from order and payer_name from proof. 4) POST /api/admin/payments/{id}/approve requires body with approver_role, updates existing order (no duplicate), sets approved_by, payer_name, payment_status=paid. 5) POST /api/admin/payments/{id}/reject requires body with reason, resets order status, handles invoice=None for guest orders without crash. 6) Marketplace cards use ProductCardCompact component with h-[52px] price block and h-[72px] action block for uniform height. 7) Both public (/marketplace) and in-account (/account/marketplace) marketplaces use identical card component.

bug_fix_applied.issue: Guest order customer_name showing as '-' in admin payments queue
bug_fix_applied.root_cause: customer_name was being set to '-' before guest_order was loaded, and the condition 'if not customer_name' was False because '-' is truthy
bug_fix_applied.fix: Changed condition to 'if not customer_name or customer_name == "-"' to properly check for unresolved customer names and use guest_order.customer_name
